name: Inedo ProGet
license: Proprietary (commercial; closed-source). The Free edition is free-of-charge and explicitly permitted for commercial use, but remains closed-source.
cost: Freemium. Free edition $0/yr; Basic $2,395/yr; Enterprise Essentials $11,995/yr; Enterprise Complete from $29,995/yr; Cloud Edition (managed SaaS) from $4,895/yr (also Edge from $59,995/yr, ISV from $11,995/yr, Inedo Suite from $4,995/yr) per inedo.com/proget/pricing, mid-2026. Licensed annually per the ProGet EULA on a per-server basis; the license includes support and maintenance.
language: C# / .NET (ASP.NET Core on Kestrel; confirmed from proget.dll and live X-ProGet-Version header). Earlier versions were ASP.NET on Windows/IIS.
last_updated: 2026-06-12 (ProGet 2026.4, build 26.0.4.10; confirmed via live X-ProGet-Version header)
perf_notes: No official published throughput/latency/memory benchmarks. Architecture is .NET (Kestrel) over PostgreSQL (embedded in 2025+ container) or SQL Server, with package files on disk or cloud object storage. ProGet exposes an internal "performance metrics" view (web requests, database calls) in the UI but no Prometheus /metrics or OpenTelemetry export endpoint (the OTel strings in the install are from bundled Microsoft/Azure libraries, not a ProGet feature). PyPI feeds cache proxied packages so resolution speed after first pull is local-disk/object-store bound.
scale_notes: Enterprise edition supports a high-availability server cluster: any number of stateless ProGet web nodes (license allows up to 5 servers/instance by default; min 3 for HA+LB) behind any load balancer (HAProxy, nginx, IIS ARR, F5, A10, Citrix). A shared encryption key enables cross-server sessions and removes the need for sticky sessions, so nodes scale horizontally; all nodes share one SQL Server (clustered/AlwaysOn recommended) and shared package storage (disk/S3/Azure). Multi-site/feed replication is available for DR and geo-distribution. HA, clustering, and multi-site replication are Enterprise-only; Free/Basic are single-instance.
summary: Inedo ProGet is a self-hosted (or cloud-managed) universal, multi-ecosystem package and container registry with a freemium model, whose PyPI feeds act as private repositories and caching proxies and layer on vulnerability scanning, license compliance, SBOMs, RBAC, and supply-chain governance.
extras: Universal multi-ecosystem registry in one product: PyPI, NuGet, npm, Maven, Docker/OCI, Helm, RubyGems, Cargo (Rust), Debian/apt, RPM/Yum, Alpine/APK, Conan (C++), Composer (PHP), Terraform, CRAN (R), Chocolatey, PowerShell, VSIX, Conda, Dart/pub, plus generic Universal/asset feeds

PyPI feed serves a PEP 503 HTML simple index at /pypi/<feed>/simple (verified with pip, plus PipEnv and Poetry); PEP 592 yank is exposed as 'Deprecation' (yanked version stays available, excluded from default installs, optional reason)
Package Vulnerability Rating System (PVRS) introduced in ProGet 2026 - contextual 1-5 real-world risk rating layered over CVSS

Software Composition Analysis (SCA): projects/builds, dependency tracking, deprecated-package alerts, compliance policies, SBOM import AND export (CycloneDX.Core bundled; SPDX/CycloneDX); pgscan generates SBOMs from .NET/Node/Python builds
Package promotion / approval workflows (unapproved->approved feeds) and quarantine of newly downloaded packages pending vulnerability scan; known-malicious-package blocking
Connector filtering and 'block noncompliant packages' policies for governance; configurable alternate /simple path for non-standard upstreams

PyPI publishing via twine (upload endpoint ends in /legacy) or pgutil; auth uses a ProGet API key (username 'api', key as password) or username/password. No OIDC trusted publishing for uploads (OpenIdConnect/SAML are for user login only)
pgutil cross-feed CLI plus extensive HTTP/Native JSON management APIs (X-ApiKey header); /health and /health/json endpoints for LB probes

Three API-key tiers: System (full or scoped to feeds/SCA/SBOM), Feed (scoped to a feed/group with View-Download / Add-Repackage / Promote / Overwrite-Delete), and Personal (impersonates user task permissions)
Custom RBAC tasks with both permissions and restrictions, three pseudogroups (Anonymous/Authenticated/Everyone), feed groups
ProGet 2025+ ships as a single Docker container with embedded PostgreSQL (no external SQL Server needed); pre-2025 requires SQL Server
Cloud Edition (SaaS) plus specialized ISV, Edge Computing, and Inedo Suite editions; webhooks plus Slack/Teams/email notifications; whitelabel/branding
sources: Live probe of public instance https://proget.inedo.com (X-ProGet-Version: 26.0.4.10) confirming version/build

Direct static analysis of ProGet 2026.4 (26.0.4.10) proget.dll: PEP 503 data-requires-python; PEP 592 data-yanked/yanked_reason; PEP 658/714 data-core-metadata=sha256; PEP 691 application/vnd.pypi.simple.v1+json and v1+html; PEP 700 upload-time JSON field; NO pypi:repository-version (PEP 629); NO targets.json/snapshot.json (PEP 458 TUF); OpenIdConnect/SAML/Ldap auth strings
